entities.name: LENTIL
entities.description: A major food crop in Ethiopia, Lens culinaris Medikus

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where lentil is a major food crop and the study is focused

entities.name: PROTEIN SOURCE
entities.description: A nutritional attribute of lentil making it an affordable protein source

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The quality of soil to provide nutrients for plant growth, sustained by nitrogen fixation

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia to sustain soil fertility

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop output per unit area, currently lower than potential for lentil in Ethiopia

entities.name: AGRONOMIC MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: The techniques used in farming to optimize crop growth and productivity

entities.name: NUTRIENT IMBALANCE
entities.description: A state where essential nutrients are not in the correct proportions for plant growth

entities.name: RHIZOBIUM
entities.description: Bacteria used as an inoculant to improve lentil growth and nitrogen fixation

entities.name: ADA'A DISTRICT
entities.description: The specific location in Ethiopia where the field experiment was conducted

entities.name: RAIN-FED CONDITION
entities.description: An agricultural condition where crops rely solely on rainfall for moisture

entities.name: SULPHUR
entities.description: A chemical element used as a nutrient fertilizer for lentil crops

entities.name: RHIZOBIUM INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of applying Rhizobium bacteria to lentil seeds or soil

entities.name: SULPHUR FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of sulphur at varying rates to improve crop yield and nutrient utilization

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The formation of nodules on the roots of the lentil plant

entities.name: NUTRIENT UTILIZATION
entities.description: The process by which the plant absorbs and uses nutrients for growth

entities.name: YIELD RESPONSE
entities.description: The change in crop yield resulting from specific treatments or inputs

entities.name: ALEMAYA LENTIL VARIETY
entities.description: The specific cultivar of lentil used as the test crop in the experiment

entities.name: RANDOMIZED COMPLETE BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The statistical experimental design used for the field trial

entities.name: DAYS TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The time taken from planting to the onset of flowering

entities.name: NUMBER OF NODULES
entities.description: The count of nodules per plant

entities.name: NODULE DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of dried nodules per plant

entities.name: NUMBER OF SEEDS PER POD
entities.description: The count of seeds within a single pod

entities.name: ABOVEGROUND DRY BIOMASS
entities.description: The total dry weight of the plant parts above the soil

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seeds produced per unit area

entities.name: SEED S UPTAKE
entities.description: The amount of sulphur absorbed by the seeds

entities.name: HAULM S UPTAKE
entities.description: The amount of sulphur absorbed by the haulm

entities.name: TOTAL S UPTAKE
entities.description: The total amount of sulphur absorbed by the plant

entities.name: SULPHUR AGRONOMIC EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency of sulphur utilization in increasing yield

entities.name: SULPHUR RECOVERY EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The percentage of applied sulphur recovered by the plant

entities.name: SULPHUR HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of seed sulphur to total plant sulphur

entities.name: NET BENEFIT
entities.description: The financial gain from lentil production after costs

entities.name: VARIABLE COST
entities.description: The costs that change with the level of production

entities.name: MARGINAL RATE OF RETURN
entities.description: An economic indicator for the profitability of the intervention
